content.raw:
AI strategy, training, community, and keynote work

Build AI capacity that serves people, communities, and culture.

I help organizations move from AI pressure to practical fluency: responsible strategy, community systems, custom training, Indigenous-tech advisory, and talks that make complicated technology feel usable without sanding off the weird, human, and political edges.

This is not generic AI consulting. It is field-building work shaped by BC+AI, the Responsible AI Professional program, and two decades of creative technology, media, and community practice.

Start a conversation Book a keynote See the work
250+BC+AI members in a growing provincial ecosystem.
3,000+BC+AI event attendees across public learning rooms.
Responsible AINew four-week certification turning AI ethics into daily practice.
20+ yearsCreative technology, media, storytelling, and community work.

How I Can Help

Five ways to work together, from focused advisory sprints to public keynotes and hands-on team training. Each lane can stand alone or combine into a larger engagement.

Strategy

AI Strategy Consulting

For: leadership teams, associations, public-interest organizations, creative companies, and teams trying to make AI decisions without getting swallowed by hype.

What we do: map opportunities, risks, tools, workflows, governance questions, and adoption patterns that fit the real organization in front of us.

  • AI readiness and opportunity map
  • Responsible adoption roadmap
  • Pilot backlog and decision language

Talk about an advisory sprint

Community

Community & Ecosystem Building

For: cities, innovation groups, regional hubs, event organizers, associations, and organizations that need more than a one-off meetup.

What we do: turn scattered interest into a functioning community system: programming, partner strategy, sponsorship, communications, membership, and rituals people want to return to.

  • Event-series and chapter architecture
  • Partner, sponsor, and member pathways
  • Community operating model and metrics

See BC+AI or ask about ecosystem work

Training

BC + AI Training

For: creative, communications, media, sales, leadership, and cross-functional teams that need hands-on AI fluency rather than another abstract trend report.

What we do: live and on-demand courses, 1:1 and group coaching, team trainings, custom workflows, and practical demos that help people use AI with judgment and taste.

  • AI mindset and workflow training
  • Role-specific prompt and tool systems
  • Team practice plans and follow-up support

Explore BC + AI training or plan a team training

Advisory

Indigenous-Tech Advisory

For: Indigenous-led organizations, public-sector teams, allies, institutions, and technology groups trying to work with data, AI, and economic evidence without extractive shortcuts.

What we do: Advisory support, drawing on my 2024–25 CTO work at Indigenomics.ai, around sovereign data, dashboard strategy, AI assistants, evidence systems, respectful technology architecture, and public storytelling.

  • Sovereign-data and governance questions
  • Dashboard and evidence-product strategy
  • AI implementation briefings and narrative systems

Read the Indigenomics AI post

Rooms

Keynotes, Workshops & Executive Briefings

For: conferences, festivals, leadership offsites, boards, associations, schools, creative communities, and teams that need the room to move.

What we do: keynote talks, panels, podcast guesting, moderation, emcee work, executive briefings, and hands-on workshops on AI, creativity, culture, ethics, and community.

  • High-voltage keynote plus Q&A
  • Keynote plus workshop packages
  • Hosting, moderation, and media appearances

See speaking topics or view the media EPK

Engagement Models

Pricing is scoped after a short discovery call because a keynote, a board briefing, a community buildout, and a training cohort are different animals. The useful question is not "what package can I sell you?" It is "what change do we need the room, team, or ecosystem to make?"

Most engagements begin with one of these shapes and then get tightened around audience, urgency, budget, and implementation needs.

Good fit when you need

  • Executive clarity on AI adoption
  • A team training that produces actual workflows
  • A public room that leaves smarter and braver
  • A community program that can keep growing
  • Protocol-aware AI and data strategy

Advisory Sprint

Two to four weeks of discovery, interviews, strategy, and a usable roadmap.

Team Training / Cohort

Half-day, full-day, or multi-week training with demos, exercises, and follow-up resources.

Ecosystem Buildout

Six to twelve weeks of program design, partner mapping, event architecture, and operating rhythms.

Keynote + Workshop

A public talk paired with a practical lab so the energy turns into action.

Proof and Source Trails

"Kris brings out the best in others and connects those around him."

Holly Rosenfeld, Seattle Cancer Care Alliance

Some of the proof is public and easy to inspect: BC+AI's current ecosystem stats, the Indigenomics AI writing and dashboard story, and the speaking archive. The rest is best handled in conversation because private executive work, client workshops, and Indigenous-tech advisory often need discretion.

  • BC+AI Ecosystem
  • Indigenomics AI article
  • Speaking page
  • Work archive
  • About Kris

Where Photography Fits Now

Visual storytelling is still part of the toolkit. I still understand rooms, faces, stages, social proof, and the value of a killer image. But photography is no longer the center of this services page.

Bring it in when it strengthens a keynote, training, community program, event, archive, or campaign. The main offer now is AI capacity, strategic clarity, community momentum, and rooms that leave with better instincts.

Start a conversation Book Kris for a keynote
